t: AI Coding — Tools, Agents & LLM APIs for Engineers
d: AI coding deep dives — Copilot vs Cursor vs Claude Code, prompt engineering, AI agents, building with LLM APIs, tool use & MCP, and AI in interviews.

How to use AI coding tools without losing control of your codebase — reviewing every diff, maintaining understanding, managing secrets and compliance, avoiding technical debt, and measuring real productivity.

t: Building with the Claude & OpenAI APIs
d: A comprehensive guide to building with the Claude and OpenAI APIs — Messages API, streaming, prompt caching, token costs, latency optimization, structured output, and security.

t: Copilot vs Cursor vs Claude Code — AI Coding Tools Compared
d: GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and Claude Code compared — how each AI coding tool works, their strengths and weaknesses, and when to use which.

t: Harness Engineering — Building Agents Around LLMs
d: Harness engineering — the discipline of building the scaffolding around an LLM that turns a text model into a capable agent: the agent loop, context assembly, tool design, memory, guardrails, verification, and evaluation.
A deep dive into how AI coding agents work — the agentic loop, tool use, ReAct pattern, context management, task planning, and self-verification.

Why the quality of your prompt determines the quality of the code — context, constraints, few-shot examples, planning-first, test-driven prompting, and the anti-patterns that silently kill output quality.

t: SDE Interviews in the AI Era
d: When AI can write boilerplate, what do SDE interviews actually test? A deep dive into the durable skills that matter more than ever: system design, debugging, code review, algorithmic intuition, and working with AI fluently.
A deep dive into function calling, tool use, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) — how models call tools, JSON schema definitions, parallel calls, and why MCP matters for agent ecosystems.
When can you use AI in a coding interview? A deep dive into where AI tools are banned, where they're allowed, how to prepare smarter with AI, and how to talk about it honestly with interviewers.
Book a 1:1 mock interview or consultation for SDE interviews. System design and coding interview prep.
Ch.1 of DDIA: what makes a system reliable, how to reason about scalability with load parameters and percentile latency, and the three pillars of maintainability.
Ch.10 of DDIA: batch processing — the Unix philosophy, MapReduce and distributed filesystems, joins and grouping, dataflow engines like Spark and Flink, and the output of batch workflows.

Ch.11 of DDIA: stream processing — event streams, message brokers, log-based vs traditional queues, change data capture, event sourcing, reasoning about time and windows, stream joins, and fault tolerance.

t: DDIA Ch.12 — The Future of Data Systems
d: Ch.12 of DDIA: the future of data systems — data integration via derived data, unbundling the database, dataflow and materialized views, end-to-end correctness, and the ethics of data.

t: DDIA Ch.2 — Data Models & Query Languages
d: Ch.2 of DDIA: relational vs document vs graph data models, impedance mismatch, schema-on-read vs schema-on-write, and declarative vs imperative query languages.
Ch.3 of DDIA: how databases store and retrieve data — log-structured storage, SSTable, LSM-tree, B-tree, secondary indexes, column stores, and OLTP vs OLAP.

Ch.4 of DDIA: how data is encoded for storage and transmission, backward vs forward compatibility, schema evolution with Protocol Buffers, Thrift, and Avro, and the three modes of dataflow — databases, services, and message passing.
Ch.5 of DDIA: replication strategies — single-leader, multi-leader, and leaderless — synchronous vs asynchronous, failover dangers, replication lag anomalies, and quorum reads/writes.
Ch.6 of DDIA: partitioning (sharding) — key-range vs hash partitioning, hot spots and skew, partitioning secondary indexes by document vs term, rebalancing, and request routing.

Ch.7 of DDIA: transactions and ACID, weak isolation levels (read committed, snapshot isolation/MVCC), lost updates, write skew and phantoms, and serializability via serial execution, two-phase locking, and SSI.

t: DDIA Ch.8 — The Trouble with Distributed Systems
d: Ch.8 of DDIA: the trouble with distributed systems — partial failure, unreliable networks and timeouts, unreliable clocks and process pauses, fencing tokens, Byzantine faults, and system models.
Ch.9 of DDIA: consistency and consensus — linearizability, the CAP theorem, ordering and causality, Lamport timestamps, total order broadcast, two-phase commit, consensus algorithms, and ZooKeeper.

t: DDIA Notes — Designing Data-Intensive Applications — updoggtech
d: Condensed notes from Designing Data-Intensive Applications (DDIA) — reliability, data models, storage engines, and more.
updoggtech — software interview prep. System design, tech stacks, and DDIA notes for SDE interviews.

t: Design Google Drive / Dropbox — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into building Google Drive / Dropbox — file chunking, content-addressed deduplication, splitting metadata from block storage, the sync algorithm, change notifications, and conflict resolution.

t: Design Nearby / Yelp (Proximity Service) — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into a proximity / nearby-places service (Yelp) — geospatial indexing with geohash and quadtrees, the boundary problem, read-heavy caching, ranking, and sharding by region.
A system design deep dive into Pastebin — key generation, separating metadata from blob storage, read-heavy caching and CDN, expiration and cleanup, and abuse prevention.

A system design deep dive into search autocomplete (typeahead) — the trie data structure, precomputed top-k suggestions, ranking, the offline data pipeline, sharding, caching, and real-time vs batch updates.

A system design deep dive into Ticketmaster — preventing double-booking with seat holds and locking, hold expiration, the virtual waiting room for onsale spikes, strong consistency for inventory, and payment integration.

t: Design a Chat App (WhatsApp) — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into building a chat app at scale — WebSocket connection management, Snowflake IDs, offline delivery, and group messaging architecture.

t: Design a Distributed Key-Value Store — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into a distributed key-value store (Dynamo-style) — consistent hashing, replication and quorums, tunable consistency, vector clocks, hinted handoff, Merkle-tree anti-entropy, and gossip membership.
A system design deep dive into building a hotel booking app — room availability tracking, oversell prevention with Redis TTL, ACID booking records, and Elasticsearch-powered search.
A system design deep dive into a real-time leaderboard — why SQL ORDER BY doesn't scale, Redis sorted sets for O(log n) rank queries, persistence, sharding, and exact vs approximate global rank.

t: Design a Map Web App (Google Maps) — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into building a map web app at scale — geospatial indexing, route pre-computation, WebSocket navigation, and ML-powered ETA refinement.

t: Design a Payment System — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into a payment system — the payment flow through a PSP, idempotency to prevent double charges, the double-entry ledger, exactly-once via reconciliation, sagas, and PCI compliance.

t: Design a Ride-Sharing App (Uber) — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into building a ride-share app — GPS ingestion, bipartite graph matching, geospatial indexing, and async payment processing.

t: Design a URL Shortener (TinyURL) — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into building a URL shortener — hashing vs. ID generation, Bloom filter collision detection, TTL purging strategies, and rate limiting.
A system design deep dive into building a video streaming platform covering upload pipelines, transcoding, CDN delivery, and ML-powered recommendations.

t: Design an E-commerce Platform — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into building a scalable e-commerce platform covering microservices, inventory, search, and event-driven order processing.

t: System Design Interview Questions & Breakdowns — updoggtech
d: System design interview prep — e-commerce, video streaming, chat app, ride-share, URL shortener and more.

A deep dive into API gateways and service meshes — handling cross-cutting concerns in microservices, north-south vs east-west traffic, the BFF pattern, the sidecar proxy model, mTLS, and gateway vs mesh.
A deep dive into Apache Kafka — pub/sub model, producers, consumers, partitions, replicas, acks, exactly-once delivery, and ZooKeeper.

A deep dive into CDNs — why edge caching cuts latency and offloads the origin, how PoPs and anycast routing work, cache-control and TTLs, push vs pull, invalidation strategies, and dynamic acceleration.

A deep dive into CI/CD — continuous integration vs delivery vs deployment, the pipeline stages, build-once immutable artifacts, deployment strategies (rolling, blue-green, canary), rollback, and quality gates.

t: Caching — Cache-Aside, Eviction Policies & Redis vs Memcached
d: A practical guide to caching — cache-aside, write-through, eviction policies, Redis vs Memcached, and how to avoid cache stampede and inconsistency.

A deep dive into DNS — how names resolve to IPs through recursive and authoritative servers, the hierarchy, record types, caching and TTL, GeoDNS and anycast for load balancing, and DNS reliability and security.
A deep dive into database indexing — why indexes exist, B-tree vs LSM-tree structures, clustered vs secondary indexes, composite and covering indexes, the leftmost-prefix rule, and when not to index.

A deep dive into Docker and containers — the dependency problem they solve, how namespaces and cgroups isolate processes, containers vs VMs, image layers, the Dockerfile, registries, and why containers won.

A deep dive into Elasticsearch — the inverted index, analyzers and tokenization, BM25 relevance scoring, shards and replicas, the distributed scatter-gather query path, near-real-time search, and queries vs filters.

t: GraphQL vs REST Comparison — Schema, Resolvers & When to Use Each
d: A deep technical comparison between GraphQL and REST — resources, API design, resolvers, and when to use each.
A deep dive into Kubernetes — pods, services, ingress, deployments, StatefulSets, master/worker architecture, namespaces, and Helm.

t: Load Balancing — Algorithms, Layer 4 vs 7, Health Checks
d: A practical guide to load balancing — algorithms, layer 4 vs layer 7, health checks, sticky sessions, and common interview tradeoffs.

t: Message Queue — Delivery Semantics, DLQ & Kafka Comparison
d: A practical guide to message queues — why they exist, delivery semantics, pull vs push, dead-letter queues, and how they compare to Kafka.

t: NoSQL — Document, Key-Value, Column-Family & CAP Theorem
d: A practical guide to NoSQL databases — document, key-value, column-family, and graph stores, CAP theorem, and when to choose NoSQL over SQL.

t: OAuth 2.0 — Authorization Code, PKCE & Grant Types Explained
d: A practical guide to OAuth 2.0 — authorization grant types, roles, protocol flow, access tokens, and how OAuth differs from authentication.

A deep dive into object storage (S3) — object vs block vs file storage, the flat namespace, the HTTP API and multipart/presigned uploads, durability via erasure coding, consistency, storage tiers, and scaling.

A deep dive into observability — monitoring vs observability, the three pillars (metrics, logs, traces), Prometheus and OpenTelemetry, distributed tracing, SLI/SLO/SLA and error budgets, and alerting on symptoms.

A deep dive into Redis — the in-memory data structure store: why it's single-threaded and fast, its data types, RDB vs AOF persistence, eviction, replication and Cluster, and the use cases that make it more than a cache.

t: Sharding — Consistent Hashing, Shard Keys & Database Partitioning
d: A practical guide to database sharding — horizontal vs vertical partitioning, sharding strategies, consistent hashing, hotspot avoidance, and cross-shard queries.

t: Spark Join Strategy — Broadcast, Sort-Merge, Shuffle Hash & Bucketing
d: A practical guide to Spark join strategies — broadcast join, shuffle hash join, sort-merge join, cartesian join, bucketing, and when to use each.

t: Tech Stacks Deep Dives — Kafka, Kubernetes, Caching & More — updoggtech
d: Tech stacks deep dives — Kafka, Kubernetes, load balancing, caching, sharding, NoSQL, OAuth, Spark, GraphQL vs REST.

A deep dive into vector databases — embeddings, similarity metrics, approximate nearest neighbor (HNSW, IVF, quantization), what a vector DB adds over a library, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and hybrid search.

A deep dive into WebSockets and real-time communication — why HTTP isn't enough, polling vs long-polling vs SSE vs WebSockets, the upgrade handshake, full-duplex connections, and scaling stateful connections with a pub/sub backplane.
A deep dive into gRPC and Protocol Buffers — contract-first RPC, the protobuf binary format, HTTP/2 foundations, the four streaming call types, code generation, and gRPC vs REST vs GraphQL.
